name: HuggingFace Tokenizers
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Rust core with Python bindings

auth.notes: Local library; HuggingFace Hub token for downloading pretrained tokenizers

performance.notes: Rust implementation; tokenizes 1GB/sec; multiple CPU cores via parallelism

agent_readiness.notes: HuggingFace's fast Rust-based tokenizer library. Implements BPE, WordPiece, Unigram, WordLevel tokenization. Used under the hood by transformers library. Load any pretrained tokenizer from Hub. Batch encoding with padding/truncation. Offset mapping for span extraction. Pre/post processing pipelines. Training new tokenizers from scratch. Python bindings around Rust core — extremely fast.

gotchas: Fast tokenizer not available for all model architectures in transformers
Offset mappings can be confusing for subword tokens
Training new tokenizers requires careful vocabulary size tuning
Some tokenizer configs are model-specific (BOS/EOS handling varies)
best_when: Fast batch tokenization for LLM preprocessing pipelines
Custom tokenizer training for domain-specific vocabularies
Span extraction tasks requiring offset mapping
avoid_when: When transformers library handles tokenization automatically (don't duplicate)
summary: HuggingFace Tokenizers is a Rust-core tokenization library with Python bindings. Implements BPE, WordPiece, and Unigram tokenization with 1GB/sec throughput. Used under the hood by the transformers library. Supports batch encoding, offset mapping, and custom tokenizer training. The go-to tokenization library for transformer model preprocessing pipelines.
